title: Boston hit 100. See what meteorologists are saying about chances for triple digits Friday and the July 4 forecast.
author: Jack Pickell
bullets: Boston hit 100°F on Thursday, marking only the 29th occurrence on record at Logan Airport since 2000.
The National Weather Service warns of dangerous heat and humidity with heat indices between 100-115°F on Friday.
Severe thunderstorms are possible Friday evening, with the greatest risk for damaging winds in western Massachusetts and Connecticut.

title: Windhorst: Celtics didn’t think Jaylen Brown was their best player last season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Celtics reportedly believed Derrick White had a better season than Jaylen Brown in 2025-26, despite Brown's strong performance.
Jaylen Brown averaged career highs in points, rebounds, and assists, leading Boston to a No. 2 seed without Jayson Tatum.
Celtics traded Jaylen Brown to the 76ers for Paul George and four draft picks amid debates over his perceived value.

title: ‘I love you JB’: 6-year-old Celtics fan’s tearful message to Jaylen Brown goes viral
author: Emily Turner
bullets: A 6-year-old Celtics fan's tearful message to Jaylen Brown gained over 840,000 likes after his trade to the 76ers.
Gio, the young fan in a Celtics jersey, expressed love and gratitude towards Brown in a viral Instagram video.
Jaylen Brown responded to the emotional post, comforting the fan by saying they would always remain friends.

title: 4 things to know about Paul George, reportedly coming to the Celtics in the Jaylen Brown trade
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The Celtics and 76ers agreed to trade Jaylen Brown for Paul George and several draft picks.
Paul George, 36, is a nine-time All-Star and played 16 NBA seasons, averaging 20.5 points per game.
George has never appeared in the NBA Finals and has faced recent durability issues, including a suspension.

title: ‘To the people of Boston, thank you’: Jaylen Brown’s statement after reportedly being traded by the Celtics
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The Boston Celtics have reportedly traded Jaylen Brown to the Philadelphia 76ers in a blockbuster deal.
Brown expressed mixed emotions, offering gratitude to Boston fans while looking forward to his move to Philadelphia.
In his statement, Brown reflected on his time in Boston, mentioning the 2024 championship and fan connections.

title: Review & setlist: It was 100 degrees in Boston, and Goose was on fire
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Goose performed a high-energy show at Boston's Leader Bank Pavilion on July 1, with mostly old favorites.
Despite the 100-degree heat, the Connecticut band thrilled fans with their signature funky, jam-heavy performances.
Goose played only one song from their new album 'Big Modern!', focusing instead on established hits.

title: Everything to know about July 4 in Boston (including how to watch the fireworks)
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: The 2026 Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular starts at 7 p.m. on July 4 with fireworks at 9:15 p.m.
Jane Lynch will host, featuring performers like Lainey Wilson and Chance The Rapper; Keith Lockhart conducts.
The event will broadcast on WHDH-TV and stream on CNN, with CNN anchors covering from the Esplanade.

title: In new podcast, former North Andover police officer Kelsey Fitzsimmons calls first judge ‘evil’ and recounts being shot
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Former North Andover officer Kelsey Fitzsimmons launches a podcast recounting being shot by a colleague and subsequent trial.
Fitzsimmons shares struggles with postpartum depression and criticizes Superior Court Judge Kathleen McCarthy-Neyman as 'evil' on the podcast.
The podcast aims to inspire others by highlighting Fitzsimmons's personal challenges and her journey of overcoming adversity.

title: Bruins’ breakout prospect Cooper Simpson has a clear path to follow in Will Zellers
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Cooper Simpson and Will Zellers, both from Minnesota, are attending the Bruins' development camp together.
Simpson, a third-round pick in the 2025 NHL Draft, scored 34 goals in the USHL last season.
Both players are set to join the University of North Dakota for the 2026-27 season.

title: New York woman indicted in alleged sex trafficking operation out of apartments in Allston, Brighton
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Zengzeng Liu, 40, of New York, indicted for operating an international sex trafficking ring in Allston and Brighton.
Liu allegedly recruited Asian women for prostitution, managing brothels and advertising on commercial sex websites.
Federal agents found nearly $105,000 in cash across two apartments and used fraudulent documents to maintain operations.
